leads.issues: Live placeholder content ('23 Something' visible in a process section), two 'Book Appointment' CTAs linking to different pages (/appointment vs /contact), no email lead capture form, reviews section promised in header but contains no actual testimonials, duplicate content blocks flagged as potential SEO penalty.
leads.opportunity: Fix the booking flow (single embedded widget, one destination) and add a lead magnet for visitors not ready to book. Real patient testimonials and local schema markup would also move the needle on Google 'chiropractor near me' rankings. Expected: 20–40% more new patient bookings within 60 days.
leads.email_subject: Your website has live placeholder text, Dr. David
leads.email_body:
Hi Dr. David,

I was looking at Independence Chiropractic's site today and noticed a couple of things that are likely costing you new patient bookings. One of your process sections still reads "23 Something" — and your two Book Appointment buttons send visitors to different pages, so the flow breaks right when someone's ready to commit.

I put together a quick page showing exactly what I'd fix and what you could realistically expect: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-06-29-independence-chiropractic.html

Happy to walk through it on a 20-minute call if it's useful — no obligation.

Gabriel Balenton
Organic Theory

leads.issues: Primary 'Request a Consultation' CTA button links to '#' (does nothing when clicked), broken SVG image placeholders throughout including attorney headshots, contact form buried at the very bottom of the page, no LocalBusiness or LegalService schema markup, Video Library referenced in nav but appears to have no content.
leads.opportunity: Fix the broken CTA immediately — it's the single highest-impact change. Moving a contact form or Calendly embed above the fold and adding local schema markup would then compound the trust signals already on the page (Super Lawyers, AVVO 10.0, 5-star reviews). Expected: 30–50% more consultation form submissions.
leads.email_subject: Your main CTA button goes nowhere — here's what I'd fix
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I came across Brittle Family Law while researching Portland family law firms. Your site has strong credentials and real Google reviews — but your primary consultation button is linked to "#", meaning it does nothing when clicked. Your contact form is also buried at the bottom, so potential clients who can't find how to reach you are likely moving on.

I put together a short page showing what I'd change and what the impact could look like: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-06-29-brittle-family-law-group.html

Happy to walk through it on a quick call if useful — no pitch, just a specific plan.

Gabriel Balenton
Organic Theory

leads.issues: 'Book Now' button redirects to an external ServiceTitan page instead of an embedded widget, multiple images failing to load (SVG placeholders visible), contact form only at page bottom with no mid-page or mobile-sticky alternative, 3,000+ five-star reviews buried in footer instead of above the fold, no FAQ schema to capture voice search and featured snippets.
leads.opportunity: Embed booking directly on the homepage and surface social proof above the fold. Emergency service customers decide in under 10 seconds — every extra click is a lost job. With an embedded widget and a floating mobile CTA, PlumbSmart would capture leads currently bouncing to simpler competitor pages. Expected: 25–40% more emergency service bookings from mobile traffic.
leads.email_subject: Your booking link sends visitors away at the worst moment
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I was reviewing PlumbSmart's site and noticed your Book Now button sends visitors to an external ServiceTitan page — breaking the experience right when someone with a burst pipe is ready to call. On mobile, where most emergency searches happen, that redirect causes a lot of people to bail.

I put together a quick page showing the specific fixes and what they'd mean for your lead volume: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-06-29-plumbsmart.html

Would be happy to walk through it on a 20-minute call if useful.

Gabriel Balenton
Organic Theory

leads.issues: Homepage title tag in Google search reads 'Contact phone Number 3393 2201' — no business name or keywords, devastating click-through rate. No meta description on any page. Broken GBP (£) currency display on an Australian clinic site (likely a rogue WooCommerce plugin). No local healthcare schema markup for opening hours or services. Missing image alt text throughout. Navigation duplicated in DOM.
leads.opportunity: Fixing the title tag and meta description alone could increase organic click-through by 20–35% without any other changes. Adding LocalBusiness/MedicalBusiness schema unlocks rich results in Google and improves Local Pack visibility. For a clinic with genuine 5-star reviews, this is low-effort, high-return work.
leads.email_subject: Your Google listing title is a phone number — here's what that's costing you
leads.email_body:
Hi Travis,

I was looking at Back To Front Chiropractic's site and noticed something that stood out straight away: when someone searches for you on Google, your homepage appears with the title "Contact phone Number 3393 2201" rather than your clinic name and specialty. That alone tanks your click-through rate and signals to Google that the page isn't properly structured.

Your site also has a currency display bug showing British pounds instead of Australian dollars, which looks broken to new visitors.

I put together a quick page showing what I'd fix and what a realistic result could look like: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-06-29-back-to-front-chiropractic.html

Happy to walk through it if useful. You can also see my work and past results at https://organic-theory.vercel.app/

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: Only 2 pages indexed by Google (homepage and a duplicate /contact-2/ URL) — the rest of the site is invisible to search engines. No meta description on any page. Footer copyright stuck at 2017, signalling an unmaintained site. H1 reads 'Welcome to ADV Partners Chartered Accountants' with no keyword targeting. Multiple competing CTAs with no clear hierarchy. Zero local SEO presence despite having two office locations.
leads.opportunity: Fixing crawl/indexation and targeting suburb-specific terms like 'tax accountant Sunshine VIC' and 'accountant Lalor' would put ADV in front of high-intent local searchers — a market with low competition and no ad spend required. Two dedicated location pages plus a corrected sitemap could realistically land them on page one within 90 days.
leads.email_subject: Google can only find 2 pages of your website
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I ran a quick index check on advaccountants.com.au and found Google has only discovered two pages of your site — the homepage and a duplicate contact page. Everything else is invisible to search engines, which means people in Sunshine and Lalor searching for an accountant are finding your competitors instead.

I put together a short page showing the specific issues and what I'd do to fix them: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-06-29-adv-accountants.html

Happy to jump on a quick call if useful. You can also see past results at https://organic-theory.vercel.app/

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: Blog contains only 3 articles, all about self-managed super funds — a niche topic that misses the much larger audience searching for general small business tax, BAS lodgements, sole trader accounting, and property investment advice. No meta description on any page, so Google writes its own snippet rather than a purposeful hook. 'Book Now' button in the header redirects to an external third-party scheduler (marvelconsulting.as.me) rather than an embedded widget, breaking the on-site experience at the moment of highest intent.
leads.opportunity: Publishing 4–6 articles targeting the searches Michael's ideal clients actually make (tax deductions for tradies, BAS lodgement guide, setting up a company in Melbourne) would pull in a consistent stream of high-intent visitors from Melbourne's western suburbs at zero ad spend. Paired with meta descriptions and an embedded booking widget, that traffic would convert into enquiries. The firm's credentials — CPA, licensed mortgage broker, 12+ years experience — are genuinely strong; the site just isn't putting them in front of the right audience.
leads.email_subject: Your website only reaches one type of client — here's who it's missing
leads.email_body:
Hi Michael,

I was looking at the Marvel Consulting site and noticed the blog only covers self-managed super funds. That's a solid niche, but it means Google has nothing to show the much bigger group searching for things like "small business accountant Footscray" or "how to reduce my tax bill as a tradie in Victoria" — all people who could become clients.

I put together a quick page showing what content would actually bring those searches to you, and what else I'd tighten up on the site: https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-06-29-marvel-consulting.html

Happy to walk through it on a short call if useful. You can also see past results at https://organic-theory.vercel.app/

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder
